task: Generate a Python function that parses messy CSV data with inconsistent delimiters and missing headers

criteria.description: Function handles the stated requirements: inconsistent delimiters, missing headers
criteria.pass_condition: Detects and handles at least 4 delimiter types (comma/tab/pipe/semicolon) with fallback chain when csv.Sniffer fails. Generates sequential column names ('col_0', 'col_1') when headers missing or invalid. Handles edge cases: empty files (returns empty data), single-column data, jagged rows (pads with None/empty strings to match max width), quoted fields with embedded delimiters, and files with only whitespace without crashing. Preserves original row structure in metadata.

sub_attributes.description: Auto-detects or handles multiple delimiter types
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if auto-detects from data, 0.5 if parameterized, 0.0 if hardcoded

sub_attributes.description: Generates headers when missing, detects when present
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if auto-detects + generates, 0.5 if one or other, 0.0 if assumes headers

sub_attributes.description: Handles empty rows, mixed quoting, trailing delimiters
sub_attributes.measurement: % of edge cases handled without crash

pass_examples: Uses csv.Sniffer for delimiter detection, heuristic for header presence
fail_examples: Hardcodes comma delimiter, assumes first row is header

criteria.description: Graceful error handling, doesn't silently corrupt data
criteria.pass_condition: Uses try/except blocks around file operations and row parsing with specific exception types (csv.Error, UnicodeDecodeError, ValueError). Never raises exceptions to caller - catches all and includes in metadata. Returns structured metadata containing: total_rows_processed (int), rows_skipped (int), delimiter_detected (str), parsing_warnings (list of {'row': int, 'issue': str, 'data': str}), and encoding_detected (str if applicable).

sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if structured error reporting, 0.5 if basic try/except, 0.0 if bare

sub_attributes.description: Never silently drops or corrupts data
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if reports all anomalies, 0.0 if silently swallows
1.0 if structured result object, 0.5 if just data, 0.0 if raw list

fail_examples: Bare except: pass, returns partial data silently

criteria.description: Function signature is clean, well-typed, with sensible defaults
criteria.pass_condition: Function signature: parse_csv(content: str, has_headers: Optional[bool] = None) -> ParseResult. Uses @dataclass for ParseResult with fields: data (List[Dict[str, str]]), headers (List[str]), metadata (Dict[str, Any]). Docstring includes: brief description, Args section with parameter descriptions, Returns section describing ParseResult structure, Raises section (should be 'None - all errors captured in metadata'), and Examples section with 2+ realistic input/output pairs showing different CSV formats.

sub_attributes.description: Full type annotations on params and return
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if complete, 0.5 if partial, 0.0 if none

sub_attributes.description: Docstring with description, args, returns, example
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if complete with example, 0.5 if basic, 0.0 if missing

sub_attributes.description: Accepts file path, string, or file object
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if multiple input types, 0.5 if one type, 0.0 if unclear

pass_examples: def parse_csv(source: str | Path | IO, ...) -> ParseResult:
fail_examples: def parse(f):  # no types, no docs

criteria.description: Uses Python idioms and stdlib appropriately
criteria.pass_condition: Uses csv.DictReader after delimiter detection (not just csv.reader). Leverages csv.Sniffer.sniff() with fallback delimiter testing. Uses dataclasses with @dataclass decorator (not TypedDict). Employs pathlib.Path.read_text() if file reading included. Uses enumerate() for row counting, list comprehensions where appropriate, and context managers for any file operations. No manual string splitting or regex parsing of CSV structure.
fail_examples: Regex-only CSV parsing, returns list of dicts with no structure

criteria.description: Code is structured for easy testing
criteria.pass_condition: Pure function with no side effects - accepts CSV content as string parameter only, with separate file reading left to caller. Logic separated into at least 2-3 helper functions (e.g., detect_delimiter, generate_headers, parse_rows). Includes concrete test examples in docstring showing expected input/output pairs for different CSV formats.

sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if pure, 0.5 if minor side effects (logging ok), 0.0 if writes files
1.0 if 3+ focused helpers, 0.5 if 2, 0.0 if monolith

sub_attributes.description: Includes example test cases or assertions
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if test cases included, 0.5 if doctest, 0.0 if none
